other_observers: Tony Leukering, Steve Messick; Peter Gent reported a Glossy Ibis at this location on 4/16/05. Doug Faulkner reported the bird the morning of 4/23/05. Greg Luger reported the bird on 4/24/05.
specific_location: Beebe Draw area, north of Weld County Road 40 between Weld County Roads 45 and 47.
description: A large, richly chestnut colored wading bird with iridescent wing feathers and a long grayish downward-curved bill. A bit larger than most of the two dozen or so White faced Ibis present. Clearly a Plegadis Ibis, this individual stood out primarily because of its dark eye, bluish white facial border that did not wrap behind the eye, and its dark bluish gray facial skin. Additionally, the legs were darker overall than the nearby White faced Ibis, typically appearing muted pinkish gray but brightening or darkening depending on the angle of the light.Behaviors: The bird was feeding in a flooded meadow with two dozen or so other Plegadis Ibis. One of the other birds appeared to be a hybrid Glossy x White faced ibis, but the rest were typical White faced Ibis.Call: not heardPlumage: adult alternate
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
